meta_title: For Freelancers: design with AI to Solve slow development speed (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome slow development speed. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Design with AI to Solve Slow Development
content.seo_description: Stop grinding through boilerplate. Learn how to leverage Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your freelance development workflow and ship high-end SaaS faster.
content.answer_block: To eliminate slow development speed, shift from manual coding to AI-assisted generation. By using Antigravity for rapid UI scaffolding and Claude Code for logic implementation, you transform your workflow from line-by-line manual entry to high-level architectural oversight, cutting development time by 70% while maintaining professional-grade code standards.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, the client is restless, and your IDE is a graveyard of half-finished components. You’re trapped in the 'boilerplate loop'—spending hours on repetitive UI setups instead of solving the actual business problems that pay the bills. The industry has shifted, yet many freelancers are still building like it’s 2015, manually typing out every div and hook.

But what if you could treat code like clay? By integrating Antigravity for visual scaffolding and Claude Code for autonomous logic implementation, you move from a laborer to an architect. This isn't just about speed; it's about reclaiming your mental bandwidth to focus on high-value features that justify your premium rates.

steps.title: Define the UI Blueprint
steps.description: Start by translating high-level business requirements into a visual structure. Use Antigravity to generate the initial component tree, ensuring the architecture is modular and scalable from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a modular React dashboard layout with a sidebar, top navigation, and a main content area. Use Tailwind CSS for styling and ensure all components are separated into a 'components/' directory.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize a project structure with the generated UI components and set up the routing logic.'

steps.title: Automate State Management
steps.description: Avoid the manual grind of writing boilerplate state hooks. Use AI to scaffold your data layer based on the UI requirements generated in the previous step.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a custom hook for user authentication and state management using Zustand. Include login, logout, and loading states.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Implement the authentication Zustand store into the AuthProvider component generated earlier.'

steps.title: Integrate API Services
steps.description: Connect your frontend to backend services without writing repetitive fetch boilerplate. Let Claude Code handle the type safety and error handling.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an API service layer that handles GET/POST requests with Axios, including base configuration and interceptors for authorization headers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review the API service and ensure all endpoints have robust error handling and TypeScript interfaces.'

steps.title: Refactor for Performance
steps.description: Once the core logic is functional, use AI to identify bottlenecks and optimize component re-renders.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current dashboard for unnecessary re-renders. Suggest memoization strategies using React.memo and useCallback.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --fix 'Apply performance optimizations based on the analysis of the dashboard components.'

steps.title: Final QA and Documentation
steps.description: Automate the creation of technical documentation and end-to-end tests to ensure the product is client-ready and maintainable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a README.md file that explains how to run the project, the folder structure, and the authentication flow. Also, create a Playwright test file for the login process.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --test 'Execute the generated Playwright test suite and report any failures.'

pro_tips: Treat your AI prompt library as your most valuable intellectual property; store them in a Notion database indexed by project type.
Always instruct your AI to 'think step-by-step' before generating code to drastically improve logic accuracy.
Use Claude Code to audit your own manual code; it often finds edge-case bugs you were too close to the project to see.
Modularize your prompts: instead of one massive prompt, feed the AI small, iterative instructions to keep the context window focused.
Prioritize type safety; tell your AI to generate Zod schemas for all API responses to prevent runtime crashes.
content.next_steps: Begin by auditing your current project's most repetitive task. Use the steps above to build a custom 'boilerplate accelerator' prompt, then integrate it into your next client sprint to measure the time saved.
content.conversion_block: Stop building the hard way. Leverage the power of AI to transform your freelance business into a high-velocity development machine. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
